# CurrentMicroframe | Apple Developer Documentation

- [ USBDriverKit ](/documentation/usbdriverkit)

- [ IOUSBHostInterface ](/documentation/usbdriverkit/iousbhostinterface)

- [ CurrentMicroframe ](/documentation/USBDriverKit/IOUSBHostInterface/CurrentMicroframe)

-  CurrentMicroframe 

Instance Method# CurrentMicroframe

DriverKit 19.0+

```
virtual kern_return_t CurrentMicroframe(uint64_t *microframeNumber, uint64_t *theTime);
```

## [ Parameters ](/documentation/USBDriverKit/IOUSBHostInterface/CurrentMicroframe#parameters)

`microframeNumber`uint64_t pointer to be updated with the current frame number

`theTime`If not NULL, this will be updated with the system time assosiated with the returned microframe

## [Return Value](/documentation/USBDriverKit/IOUSBHostInterface/CurrentMicroframe#return-value)

KERN_SUCCESS is successful see IOReturn.h for error codes.

## [Discussion](/documentation/USBDriverKit/IOUSBHostInterface/CurrentMicroframe#discussion)

Return the current microframe number of the USB controller

This method will return the current microframe number of the USB controller. This is most useful for scheduling future isochronous requests.
